titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the tatiana de la tierra's writing, 1996-2003.
titlestmt.author: Compiled by tatiana de la tierra

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library

titlepage.titleproper: Guide to the Tatiana De La Tierra's Writing,1996-2003.
titlepage.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections Cornell University Library

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu
http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

titlepage.date: © 2003 Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library

did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY
did.repository: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library
did.abstract: Self-published collections of her writings, copies of published articles and chapters, some of her unpublished pieces, and a collection of flyers and posters, mostly on Latina lesbian themes.
did.langmaterial: Collection material in English and Spanish

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: tatiana de la tierra was one of the founders and editors of the Latina lesbian magazines, Esto No Tienne Nombre and Conmocion. Her writings have appeared in periodicals and anthologies since 1987. In 2002, she published the book For The Hard Ones: A Lesbian Phenomenology.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Self-published collections of her writings, copies of published articles and chapters, some of her unpublished pieces, and a collection of flyers and posters, mostly on Latina lesbian themes.

controlaccess.head: Subjects:
controlaccess.subject: Hispanic American lesbians--Intellectual life.
Lesbians' writings, Hispanic American (Spanish)--History and criticism.
Lesbians' writings, Latin American--History and criticism.
Erotic stories, Hispanic American.
Women and erotica.
Lesbians' writings, Latina.

did.unittitle: "Activist Latina Lesbian Publishing: esto no tienne nombre and conmocion," by de la tierra
"A Sky That Was Only Sky," self-published collection of previously published writings by de la tierra
"Coming Out and Creating Queer Awareness in the Classroom: An Approach from the US-Mexican Border," by de la tierra
"Latina Lesbian Literary Herstory: From Sor Juana to Days of Awe," by de la tierra
Sangre Sobre Las Rosas, by de la tierra

scopecontent.p: A collection of short stories, poems, her first theatrical work, etc., unpublished

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: tatiana de la tierra's writing, #7710. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
